validator.description: Student selected 75, incorrect. Added Ava and Noah instead of finding the difference.

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlight.

beats.text: You added them. The question asks how many MORE. That means subtract to find the difference.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: The portion of Ava's bar that extends beyond Noah's bar highlights, showing the 25-unit difference between 50 and 25.

beats.text: Let me show you how to find the difference. Ava's bar ends at 50. Noah's bar ends at 25. To find how many MORE, I subtract: 50 minus 25 equals 25. The answer is 25 more miles.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Miles Walked This Week showing Kai=35, Ava=50, Noah=25, Zoe=45. Ava and Noah bars highlighted.

params.description: Vertical data table appears. Plants in the Garden data. Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20.
Horizontal bar graph appears in building mode. Plants in the Garden title. Scale of 10, axis 0–70. No bars filled. Ghost gridlines at 5-unit intervals visible.

beats.text: Use the data table to complete the graph.
Create the bar graph.

validator.description: Student set all four bars correctly: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20

params.description: All four bars confirm simultaneously.

validator.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

beats.text: Check the data table. Make sure each bar matches the number.

validator.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: All categories highlight in data table.

beats.text: Look at the ghost gridlines. They help you see where halfway is. Tomatoes is 60, right at the line. Peppers is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Find where each number goes.

validator.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting each bar sequentially: Tomatoes to 60, Peppers to 35, Carrots to 45, Beans to 20.

beats.text: Here's how it works. Tomatoes is 60, at the 60 line. Peppers is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Carrots is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Beans is 20, right at the 20 line. When you read the data table, you find each value on the axis and set the bar there.

elements.description: Vertical data table. Plants in the Garden data. Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20.
Horizontal bar graph in building mode. All four bars complete: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20.

params.description: Data table hides from screen.
Bar graph switches to reading mode. Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

beats.text: How many plants in all for Peppers and Carrots?
How many plants IN ALL for Peppers and Carrots?

validator.description: Student selected 80, correct total for Peppers + Carrots

beats.text: Right. 80 plants in all. 35 plus 45 is 80.

validator.description: Student selected 70, possibly reading one category value instead of adding

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

beats.text: The question asks for the total. Find both values on the graph, then add them together. What is 35 plus 45?

validator.description: Student selected 75, close but incorrect sum

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

beats.text: Check your addition. Peppers is at 35, Carrots is at 45. Add those two numbers carefully.

validator.description: Student selected 90, possible counting error or misreading bars

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

beats.text: Read each bar carefully. Peppers ends at 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Carrots ends at 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Now add those.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System traces Peppers bar to 35 on axis, then Carrots bar to 45 on axis. Both values glow.

beats.text: Let me show you. Peppers is at 35. Carrots is at 45. To find the total, I add 35 plus 45. That equals 80. The answer is 80 plants in all.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Peppers and Carrots bars highlighted. All bars complete: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20.

params.title: Plants in the Garden
params.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Plants in the Garden. Four bars: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20. Scale of 10, axis range 0–70.

beats.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You used the axis to find values, even values between the lines. Let's see what you know.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Plants in the Garden with four completed bars: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20. Scale of 10, axis range 0–70.
